name: raylog-markdown-tasks
title: Raylog - Markdown Tasks
description: Manage focused local tasks from Raycast with a standalone markdown note.
author: dillon-mccardell

preferences.name: storageNotePath
preferences.title: Storage Note
preferences.description: Markdown note used for Raylog storage
preferences.label: Storage Note

commands.name: list-tasks
commands.title: List Tasks
commands.description: Focus, filter, and move Raylog tasks through their lifecycle

preferences.name: showDueDateIndicator
preferences.title: Due Countdown
preferences.description: Display the due date countdown in the task list
preferences.label: Display due countdowns in the task list

preferences.name: showPastDueIndicator
preferences.title: Past Due Indicator
preferences.description: Display overdue tasks with a past due indicator in the task list
preferences.label: Highlight overdue tasks in the task list

preferences.name: showStartDateIndicator
preferences.title: Start Countdown
preferences.description: Display the start date countdown in the task list
preferences.label: Display start countdowns in the task list

preferences.name: dueSoonDays
preferences.title: Due Soon Days
preferences.description: Number of days for a task to be considered Due Soon
preferences.label: Days ahead

preferences.name: logStatusBehavior
preferences.title: Status Change on Log
preferences.description: Choose what happens when logging work on a to-do task

commands.name: menu-bar-task
commands.title: Refresh Menu Bar
commands.description: Show the next Raylog task in the macOS menu bar.

commands.name: add-task
commands.title: Add Task
commands.description: Capture a new Raylog task with dates and status.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
